info.name: AbuseIPDB APIv2
info.description:
AbuseIPDB APIv2 is a REST API for querying IP reputation, downloading the
community blacklist, submitting single and bulk abuse reports, checking
CIDR network ranges, and clearing your own past reports. All requests are
authenticated with an API key supplied via the `Key` HTTP header and must
be made over HTTPS. Responses are returned as JSON unless otherwise noted.


Contact Support:
 Name: AbuseIPDB Support

query.description: (Required) The IPv4 or IPv6 address to check.
Restrict reports considered to those within the last N days (1-365, default 30).
When present, include the most recent reports in the response.

request.description:
Returns the current abuse data for a single IPv4 or IPv6 address,
including the abuse confidence score, country, ISP, usage type, and
(when `verbose` is set) the most recent reports.

response.name: Abuse data for the requested IP.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: Minimum confidence score (25-100, default 100; subscriber feature below 100).
Maximum number of entries to return (varies by plan, up to 500,000).
Comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes to include (subscriber feature).
Comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes to exclude (subscriber feature).
Restrict to a specific IP version (4 or 6).
When present, return one IP per line as text/plain instead of JSON.

request.description:
Returns the AbuseIPDB blacklist. Free accounts receive up to 10,000
entries; subscriber tiers can customise the confidence floor, country
filters, IP version, and maximum result count.

urlencoded.description: (Required) IPv4 or IPv6 address to report.
(Required) Comma-separated category IDs (up to 30).
Optional description and supporting log evidence.
Optional ISO 8601 timestamp when the abuse occurred.

request.description:
Submit a new abuse report for an IP address with one or more category
IDs, an optional comment, and an optional ISO 8601 timestamp.

query.description: (Required) A CIDR network range (e.g. `192.0.2.0/24`).
Restrict to reports within the last N days (1-365, default 30).

request.description:
Returns the abuse data for a CIDR network range. Free accounts may query
ranges up to /24; subscriber tiers can query up to /16.

response.name: Abuse data for the network range.
